headline: Deterministic fest orchestrator: Python decision-tree picks the next festival task, runs lightweight agents for free, escalates heavyweight/ambiguous work to Claude. No LLM in the loop.

capabilities.label: Festival status: phase completion + remaining work (read-only)
capabilities.notes: orchestrator.py lives OUTSIDE this repo (festival-project, not Pre Atlas) -- invoke is an absolute cross-repo path, same pattern as any other trusted cli overlay command. Optional `festival` overrides the default festival dir (--festival <path>); omit to use the orchestrator's own default.

capabilities.label: Full plan: agent assignments for every remaining task (read-only)
Preview the next task's agent assignment without executing it (read-only)
Execute the next festival task via the decision tree (runs a lightweight agent, or escalates)
capabilities.notes: Runs ONE task then stops (never loops -- that's /autopilot go, a Claude-side loop over repeated calls to this capability, not a single CLI invocation, so it isn't declared as its own capability here). Never auto-marks a task done -- the caller (Claude) verifies results and updates the task file. Returns structured JSON: {action, reason, agent, task}; action='escalate' means the caller must do the work itself.
